Car plus l'insouciance s'empare du cœur, plus sa dureté augmente. Lorsque l'on évoque Allah, le Très-Haut, cette dureté fond, comme le plomb fond dans le feu.
- Le rappel implique la prière (bénédiction) d'Allah, Puissant et Majestueux, et de Ses anges sur celui qui l'évoque. Celui qu'Allah, le Très-Haut, et Ses anges bénissent a certes réussi pleinement et gagné toute victoire. Il dit, Gloire et Pureté à Lui : "Ô vous qui avez cru ! Invoquez Allah d'une invocation abondante, et glorifiez-Le à la pointe du jour et à la tombée de la nuit. C'est Lui qui prie sur vous, ainsi que Ses anges, pour vous faire sortir des ténèbres à la lumière ; et Il est Miséricordieux envers les croyants."
- Les assemblées de rappel sont les assemblées des anges. Il n'y a pas d'assemblée mondaine qui leur soit destinée, sauf une assemblée où Allah, le Très-Haut, est évoqué. Comme il est rapporté dans les deux Sahih, d'après Al-A'mash, d'après Abi Salih, d'après Abou Hourayra, que le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Allah a des anges qui parcourent les routes à la recherche des gens du rappel. Lorsqu'ils trouvent des gens qui évoquent Allah, le Très-Haut, ils s'appellent les uns les autres : 'Venez à ce que vous cherchiez !' Ils les entourent alors de leurs ailes jusqu'au ciel le plus proche. Leur Seigneur, le Très-Haut, leur demande, bien qu'Il soit plus savant qu'eux : 'Que disent Mes serviteurs ?' Ils répondent : 'Ils Te glorifient, Te proclament grand et Te louent.' Il dit : 'M'ont-ils vu ?' Ils disent : 'Non, par Allah, ils ne T'ont pas vu.' Il dit : 'Comment serait-ce s'ils Me voyaient ?' Ils disent : 'S'ils Te voyaient, ils T'adoreraient avec plus d'ardeur, Te loueraient avec plus d'ardeur, Te magnifieraient avec plus d'ardeur et Te glorifieraient davantage.' Il dit : 'Que Me demandent-ils ?' Ils disent : 'Ils Te demandent le Paradis.' Il dit : 'L'ont-ils vu ?' Ils disent : 'Non, par Allah, Seigneur, ils ne l'ont pas vu.' Il dit : 'Comment serait-ce s'ils le voyaient ?' Ils disent : 'S'ils le voyaient, ils y aspireraient plus ardemment, le rechercheraient avec plus d'ardeur et y seraient plus attachés.' Il dit : 'De quoi cherchent-ils refuge ?' Ils disent : 'Du Feu.' Il dit : 'L'ont-ils vu ?' Ils disent : 'Non, par Allah, Seigneur, ils ne l'ont pas vu.' Il dit : 'Comment serait-ce s'ils le voyaient ?' Ils disent : 'S'ils le voyaient, ils le fuiraient plus ardemment et en auraient plus peur.' Il dit : 'Je vous prends à témoins que Je leur ai pardonné.' Un ange dit alors : 'Parmi eux se trouve untel, qui n'est pas des leurs, il est venu pour un besoin.' Il dit : 'Ce sont des compagnons tels que leur compagnon ne saurait être malheureux.'" Telle est leur bénédiction sur eux-mêmes et sur leur compagnon. Ils ont une part de Sa parole : "Et fait que je sois béni partout où je serai." Ainsi en est-il du croyant, béni partout où il se trouve, alors que le pervers est funeste partout où il se trouve. Les assemblées de rappel sont les assemblées des anges, et les assemblées d'insouciance sont les assemblées des diables. Chacun est attiré vers ce qui lui ressemble, et l'homme finit par rejoindre ce qui lui correspond.
- Allah, Puissant et Majestueux, tire fierté de ceux qui L'évoquent devant Ses anges, comme l'a rapporté Muslim dans son Sahih d'après Abou Sa'id al-Khudri : Mu'awiya sortit et vit un cercle (de gens) dans la mosquée. Il dit : "Qu'est-ce qui vous fait asseoir ?" Ils dirent : "Nous nous sommes assis pour évoquer Allah, le Très-Haut." Il dit : "Par Allah, est-ce uniquement cela qui vous fait asseoir ?" Ils dirent : "Par Allah, cela seul nous fait asseoir." Il dit : "Je ne vous ai pas fait prêter serment parce que je vous soupçonne, mais personne n'est, de par sa position auprès du Messager d'Allah, moins rapporteur de hadith que moi. Le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) sortit et vit un cercle de ses Compagnons. Il dit : 'Qu'est-ce qui vous fait asseoir ?' Ils dirent : 'Nous nous sommes assis pour évoquer Allah, le Très-Haut, et Le louer de nous avoir guidés à l'Islam et de nous avoir favorisés par toi.' Il dit : 'Par Allah, est-ce uniquement cela qui vous fait asseoir ?' Ils dirent : 'Par Allah, cela seul nous fait asseoir.' Il dit : 'Je ne vous ai pas fait prêter serment parce que je vous soupçonne, mais Gabriel est venu m'informer qu'Allah, le Béni et Exalté, tire fierté de vous auprès des anges.'" Cette fierté du Seigneur, Béni et Exalté, est une preuve de l'honneur du rappel auprès de Lui, de Son amour pour lui, et qu'il a une supériorité sur les autres actes d'adoration.
- Tous les actes d'adoration n'ont été institués que pour établir le rappel d'Allah, le Très-Haut, et leur but est de réaliser le rappel d'Allah, le Très-Haut. Il dit, Gloire et Pureté à Lui : "Accomplis la prière pour te souvenir de Moi."
- Le rappel d'Allah, Puissant et Majestueux, facilite ce qui est difficile, rend aisé ce qui est ardu, et allège les peines. Il n'est pas de chose difficile sur laquelle on évoque Allah sans qu'elle ne devienne aisée, ni d'obstacle sans qu'il ne soit levé, ni de peine sans qu'elle ne s'allège, ni de difficulté sans qu'elle ne disparaisse, ni d'angoisse sans qu'elle ne se dissipe. Le rappel d'Allah, le Très-Haut, est l'issue après l'épreuve, la facilité après la difficulté, la délivrance après l'angoisse et le souci.
- L'abondance du rappel d'Allah, Puissant et Majestueux, est une garantie contre l'hypocrisie. Les hypocrites n'évoquent qu' peu Allah, Puissant et Majestueux. Allah, le Très-Haut, dit à propos des hypocrites : "et ils n'évoquent Allah que rarement." Ka'b a dit : "Celui qui évoque beaucoup Allah, Puissant et Majestueux, est préservé de l'hypocrisie." C'est pourquoi, et Allah sait mieux, Allah, le Très-Haut, a conclu la sourate des Hypocrites par Sa parole : "Ô vous qui avez cru ! Que vos biens et vos enfants ne vous distraient pas du rappel d'Allah. Et quiconque fait cela... alors ceux-là seront les perdants." Il y a là un avertissement contre la tentation des hypocrites qui se sont désintéressés du rappel d'Allah, Puissant et Majestueux, et sont ainsi tombés dans l'hypocrisie.
text.en:
**The Virtue of Remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah** Indeed, among the best and easiest righteous deeds that bring a Muslim closer to his Lord, Almighty and Majestic, is the remembrance (Dhikr) of Allah. **Verses and Hadiths on the Virtue of Remembrance (Dhikr) and Supplication, and Encouragement to Practice Them:**
Numerous verses and authentic hadiths from the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) have been reported on this topic. We mention what is easy for us:
- The Most High says: "And the remembrance of Allah is surely greatest."
- He says: "And exalt Him morning and afternoon."
- He says: "Remember Me, and I will remember you. Be grateful to Me and do not deny Me."
- He says: "O you who have believed, remember Allah with much remembrance."
- He says: "and the men who remember Allah often and the women who do so - for them Allah has prepared forgiveness and a great reward."
- He says: "And remember your Lord much and exalt [Him with praise] in the evening and the morning."
- He says: "Who remember Allah while standing or sitting or [lying] on their sides."
- He says: "And when you have completed your rites, remember Allah like your [previous] remembrance of your fathers or with [much] greater remembrance."
- He says: "O you who have believed, let not your wealth and your children divert you from the remembrance of Allah. And whoever does that - then those are the losers."
- He says: "And remember your Lord within yourself in humility and in fear without being apparent in speech - in the mornings and the evenings. And do not be among the heedless."
- The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "The example of the one who remembers his Lord and the one who does not remember Him is like that of the living and the dead."
- The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Shall I not tell you of the best of your deeds, the most pure to your Sovereign, the one that raises your ranks the highest, that is better for you than spending gold and silver, and better for you than meeting your enemies, striking their necks and they strike your necks?" They said, "Yes." He said, "It is the remembrance of Allah, the Most High."
- The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Allah, the Most High, says: 'I am as My servant thinks of Me, and I am with him when he remembers Me. If he remembers Me to himself, I remember him to Myself; if he remembers Me in a gathering, I remember him in a gathering better than his. If he draws near to Me by a handspan, I draw near to him by a cubit; if he draws near to Me by a cubit, I draw near to him by a fathom; if he comes to Me walking, I come to him running.'"
- The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Whoever sits in a place and does not remember Allah therein, will incur a loss (deficiency) from Allah. And whoever lies down in a place and does not remember Allah therein, will incur a loss from Allah."
- The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "No people sit in a gathering and do not remember Allah therein nor send blessings upon their Prophet, except that it will be a source of regret for them. If He wills, He will punish them, and if He wills, He will forgive them."
- The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "No people get up from a gathering in which they did not remember Allah, except that it is as if they got up from the carcass of a donkey, and it will be a source of grief for them." **The Virtues of Remembrance (Dhikr) according to Imam Ibn Qayyim al-Jawziyya:**
- It repels Satan, suppresses, and breaks him.
- It pleases the Most Merciful, Almighty and Majestic.
- It removes worry and grief from the heart.
- It brings joy, happiness, and expansiveness to the heart.
- It attracts provision.
- It clothes the one who remembers (Allah) with dignity, sweetness, and radiance.
- It bequeaths to him love (of Allah), which is the spirit of Islam, the pivot of the religion, and the center of happiness and salvation. Allah has made perseverance in remembrance the cause of love. Whoever desires to attain the love of Allah, Almighty and Majestic, let him devote himself to His remembrance, for it is the lesson and study, just as it is the door to knowledge. Remembrance is the door to love, its greatest avenue, and its straightest path.
- It bequeaths to him vigilance (muraqaba) until it enters him into the station of excellence (Ihsan), where he worships Allah as if he sees Him. The heedless has no way to this station, just as the one who is sitting cannot reach the house.
- It bequeaths to him penitent return (Inaba) to Allah, Almighty and Majestic. The more he returns to Him through His remembrance, the more it makes his heart return to Him in all circumstances. Allah then becomes his refuge, his shelter, his asylum, the qibla of his heart, and his escape during calamities and trials.
- It bequeaths to him reverential fear (hayba) of his Lord, Almighty and Majestic, and veneration of Him, due to the strength of His dominion over his heart and his presence with Allah, contrary to the heedless whose veil of fear is thin in his heart.
- It earns him that Allah, the Most High, remembers him, as He says: "Remember Me, and I will remember you." And if there were no other virtue in remembrance than this alone, it would suffice as merit and honor. And He says in what He reports from His Lord, Blessed and Exalted: "Whoever remembers Me to himself, I remember him to Myself; whoever remembers Me in a gathering, I remember him in a gathering better than his." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim).
- It removes sins and makes them disappear, for it is among the greatest good deeds, and good deeds erase bad deeds.
- It removes the estrangement between the servant and his Lord, Blessed and Exalted. The heedless has a distance from Allah, Almighty and Majestic, which is only removed by remembrance.
- When the servant makes himself known to Allah, the Most High, through His remembrance in ease, He recognizes him in adversity. An effect to this meaning has been reported: When the obedient servant who remembers Allah is struck by hardship or asks Allah for something, the angels say: "Lord, it is a known voice from a known servant." While when the heedless one who turns away from Allah calls upon Him and asks Him, the angels say: "Lord, it is an unknown voice from an unknown servant." And the hadith of Ibn Abbas: "Recognize Allah in times of ease, and He will recognize you in times of hardship." (Reported by Imam Ahmad).
- It saves from the punishment of Allah, the Most High, as Mu'adh (may Allah be pleased with him) said, and it is reported as a raised (marfu') hadith: "No human being has done any deed more saving from the punishment of Allah, Almighty and Majestic, than the remembrance of Allah, the Most High." (Reported by Ahmad, Malik, Ibn Majah, and At-Tirmidhi).
- It is the cause of the descent of tranquility (as-sakina), the enveloping of mercy, and the surrounding of the angels around the one who remembers (Allah), as the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) announced in the hadith: "No people sit remembering Allah, except that the angels surround them, mercy envelops them, tranquility descends upon them, and Allah mentions them among those who are with Him." (Reported by Muslim).
- It is a cause of occupying the tongue, thus protecting it from backbiting, slander, lying, obscenity, and futility. The servant must inevitably speak. If he does not speak by remembering Allah, the Most High, and mentioning His commands, he will speak about these forbidden things, or some of them. There is no way to be safe from them at all except through the remembrance of Allah, the Most High. Observation and experience testify to this. Whoever accustoms his tongue to the remembrance of Allah protects his tongue from falsehood and vanity; and whoever's tongue dries up from the remembrance of Allah, the Most High, becomes moist with every lie, vanity, and obscenity. There is no power nor might except with Allah.
- It secures the servant from regret on the Day of Resurrection. Every gathering in which the servant does not remember his Lord, the Most High, will be a source of regret and loss for him on the Day of Resurrection.
- It is the easiest act of worship, while being among the noblest and best. The movement of the tongue is the lightest and easiest of the movements of the limbs. If a limb of the human body moved as much as the tongue in a day and night, it would be extremely difficult for him, rather impossible. And in the hadith: "Two words light on the tongue, heavy in the scale, beloved to the Most Merciful: 'Glory and praise be to Allah, glory be to Allah the Magnificent (Subhana Allah wa bihamdihi, Subhana Allah al-'Adhim).'"
- It is the plantation of Paradise. In the collection of At-Tirmidhi, from Abu Az-Zubair, from Jabir, the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Whoever says: 'Glory and praise be to Allah (Subhana Allah wa bihamdihi)', a palm tree is planted for him in Paradise." At-Tirmidhi said this hadith is good and authentic, and Al-Albani authenticated it in Sahih Al-Jami' (no. 6429).
- The reward and merit attached to it are not attached to any other deed. In the two Sahihs, from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Whoever says one hundred times in a day: 'There is no deity worthy of worship except Allah, alone, without partner; to Him belongs all sovereignty and praise, and He is over all things Omnipotent,' will have a reward equivalent to freeing ten slaves, one hundred good deeds will be recorded for him, one hundred bad deeds will be erased from him, and it will be a protection for him from Satan during that day until evening. No one will do better than him except someone who does more. And whoever says one hundred times in a day: 'Glory and praise be to Allah (Subhana Allah wa bihamdihi),' his sins will be forgiven even if they were like the foam of the sea." And in Sahih Muslim, from Abu Hurairah, the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "To say: 'Glory be to Allah, praise be to Allah, there is no deity but Allah, Allah is the Greatest' is more beloved to me than everything upon which the sun rises."
- Persistence in the remembrance of the Lord, Blessed and Exalted, guarantees safety from forgetting Him, which is the cause of the servant's misery in his present life and his final return. Forgetting the Lord, Glorified and Exalted, entails forgetting oneself and one's own interests. He, the Most High, says: "And be not like those who forgot Allah, so He made them forget themselves. Those are the defiantly disobedient." When the servant forgets himself, he turns away from his interests, forgets them, and busies himself elsewhere, which inevitably leads to his ruin and corruption. Whoever forgets Allah, the Most High, Allah will make him forget himself in this world and will forget him in the punishment on the Day of Resurrection. He, the Most High, says: "And whoever turns away from My remembrance - indeed, he will have a depressed [i.e., difficult] life, and We will gather [i.e., raise] him on the Day of Resurrection blind. He will say, 'My Lord, why have you raised me blind while I was [once] seeing?' [Allah] will say, 'Thus did Our signs come to you, and you forgot them; and thus will you this day be forgotten.'" That is, you will be forgotten in the punishment, just as you forgot My signs, not remembering them and not acting upon them.
- Remembrance is a light for the one who remembers in this world, a light in his grave, and a light for his return, running before him on the bridge (as-sirat). Nothing illuminates hearts and graves like the remembrance of Allah, the Most High. Allah, the Most High, says: "Is one who was dead and We gave him life and made for him a light by which to walk among the people like one who is in darkness, never to emerge therefrom?" The first is the believer who was illuminated by faith in Allah, His love, His knowledge, and His remembrance; the other is the heedless of Allah, the Most High, who turns away from His remembrance and His love. All honor, all success lies in the light; all misery lies in its loss.
- Remembrance gathers what is scattered, scatters what is gathered, brings near what is far, and distances what is near.
- In the heart there is a hardness that only the remembrance of Allah, the Most High, can melt. The servant should therefore remedy the hardness of his heart with the remembrance of Allah, the Most High. Hammad ibn Zayd reported from Al-Mu'alla ibn Ziyad that a man said to Al-Hasan: "O Abu Sa'id, I complain to you of the hardness of my heart." He replied: "Melt it with remembrance (of Allah)." Because the more heedlessness seizes the heart, the harder it becomes. When one remembers Allah, the Most High, this hardness melts, like lead melts in fire.
- Remembrance entails the prayer (blessing) of Allah, Almighty and Majestic, and His angels upon the one who remembers. Whoever Allah, the Most High, and His angels bless has certainly succeeded completely and won every victory. He, Glory and Purity to Him, says: "O you who have believed, remember Allah with much remembrance and exalt Him morning and afternoon. It is He who confers blessing upon you, and His angels [ask Him to do so] to bring you out from darknesses into the light. And ever is He, to the believers, Merciful."
- The gatherings of remembrance are the gatherings of the angels. There is no worldly gathering that is for them, except a gathering where Allah, the Most High, is remembered. As reported in the two Sahihs, from Al-A'mash, from Abu Salih, from Abu Hurairah, that the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Allah has angels who roam the roads seeking the people of remembrance. When they find people who remember Allah, the Most High, they call out to one another: 'Come to what you are seeking!' They then surround them with their wings up to the nearest heaven. Their Lord, the Most High, asks them, although He is more knowledgeable than them: 'What are My servants saying?' They reply: 'They glorify You, proclaim You great, and praise You.' He says: 'Have they seen Me?' They say: 'No, by Allah, they have not seen You.' He says: 'How would it be if they saw Me?' They say: 'If they saw You, they would worship You with more fervor, praise You with more fervor, magnify You with more fervor, and glorify You more.' He says: 'What do they ask Me for?' They say: 'They ask You for Paradise.' He says: 'Have they seen it?' They say: 'No, by Allah, Lord, they have not seen it.' He says: 'How would it be if they saw it?' They say: 'If they saw it, they would yearn for it more intensely, seek it more ardently, and be more attached to it.' He says: 'What do they seek refuge from?' They say: 'From the Fire.' He says: 'Have they seen it?' They say: 'No, by Allah, Lord, they have not seen it.' He says: 'How would it be if they saw it?' They say: 'If they saw it, they would flee from it more intensely and fear it more.' He says: 'I call you to witness that I have forgiven them.' One angel then says: 'Among them is so-and-so, who is not one of them; he came for a need.' He says: 'They are such that their companion is not wretched.'" This is their blessing upon themselves and their companion. They have a share in His words: "And make me blessed wherever I am." Thus is the believer, blessed wherever he is, while the wicked is ill-omened wherever he is. The gatherings of remembrance are the gatherings of the angels, and the gatherings of heedlessness are the gatherings of devils. Each is drawn to what resembles him, and a person ends up with what is fitting for him.
- Allah, Almighty and Majestic, boasts of those who remember Him before His angels, as Muslim reported in his Sahih from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri: Mu'awiyah came out and saw a circle (of people) in the mosque. He said: "What makes you sit?" They said: "We have sat to remember Allah, the Most High." He said: "By Allah, is that alone what makes you sit?" They said: "By Allah, that alone makes us sit." He said: "I did not make you swear because I suspect you, but no one, by virtue of his position with the Messenger of Allah, narrates fewer hadiths than I. The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) came out and saw a circle of his Companions. He said: 'What makes you sit?' They said: 'We have sat to remember Allah, the Most High, and praise Him for guiding us to Islam and favoring us with you.' He said: 'By Allah, is that alone what makes you sit?' They said: 'By Allah, that alone makes us sit.' He said: 'I did not make you swear because I suspect you, but Gabriel came and informed me that Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, boasts of you before the angels.'" This boasting of the Lord, Blessed and Exalted, is proof of the honor of remembrance with Him, His love for it, and that it has superiority over other acts of worship.
- All acts of worship were only prescribed to establish the remembrance of Allah, the Most High, and their purpose is to achieve the remembrance of Allah, the Most High. He, Glory and Purity to Him, says: "Establish prayer for My remembrance."
- The remembrance of Allah, Almighty and Majestic, makes what is difficult easy, what is hard easy, and lightens burdens. There is no difficult thing upon which Allah is remembered without it becoming easy, no obstacle without it being removed, no pain without it being lightened, no hardship without it disappearing, no distress without it being relieved. The remembrance of Allah, the Most High, is the outcome after tribulation, the ease after difficulty, the deliverance after anguish and worry.
- Abundance of the remembrance of Allah, Almighty and Majestic, is a safeguard against hypocrisy. The hypocrites remember Allah, Almighty and Majestic, only a little. Allah, the Most High, says about the hypocrites: "and they do not remember Allah except a little." Ka'b said: "Whoever remembers Allah, Almighty and Majestic, much is safe from hypocrisy." This is why, and Allah knows best, Allah, the Most High, concluded Surah Al-Munafiqun (The Hypocrites) with His words: "O you who have believed, let not your wealth and your children divert you from the remembrance of Allah. And whoever does that - then those are the losers." In this is a warning against the temptation of the hypocrites who were heedless of the remembrance of Allah, Almighty and Majestic, and thus fell into hypocrisy.

notes.fr: Ceci est un texte explicatif détaillé sur le mérite du rappel (Dhikr), compilé à partir de versets, hadiths et paroles d'érudits. Le champ 'repeat' est à 0 car il ne s'agit pas d'une invocation à répéter.
notes.en: This is a detailed explanatory text on the virtue of remembrance (Dhikr), compiled from verses, hadiths, and sayings of scholars. The 'repeat' field is 0 as it is not a supplication to be repeated.

text.ar: فَضْلُ سُورَةِ الفَاتِحَة الأحاديث الصحيحة التي وردت فى فضل سورة الفاتحة عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدِ بْنِ المُعَلَّى رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ لِيَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: أَلاَ أُعَلِّمُكَ أَعْظَمَ سُورَةٍ فِي القُرْآنِ قَبْلَ أَنْ أَخْرُجَ مِنَ المَسْجِدِ". فَذَهَبَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِيَخْرُجَ مِنَ المَسْجِدِ فَذَكَّرْتُهُ، فَقَالَ: "(الحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ العَالَمِينَ). هِيَ السَّبْعُ المَثَانِي، وَالقُرْآنُ العَظِيمُ الَّذِي أُوتِيتُهُ». رَوَاه البُخَارِي (6/ 4474)، وأَحمَد (24/ 15730). وعَن عَبْدِ اللهِ بْنِ عَبَّاسٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمَا، قَالَ: بَيْنَمَا جِبْرِيلُ قَاعِدٌ عِنْدَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، سَمِعَ نَقِيضًا مِنْ فَوْقِهِ، فَرَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ، فَقَالَ: "هَذَا بَابٌ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ فُتِحَ الْيَوْمَ لَمْ يُفْتَحْ قَطُّ إِلَّا الْيَوْمَ، فَنَزَلَ مِنْهُ مَلَكٌ، فَقَالَ: هَذَا مَلَكٌ نَزَلَ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ لَمْ يَنْزِلْ قَطُّ إِلَّا الْيَوْمَ، فَسَلَّمَ، وَقَالَ: أَبْشِرْ بِنُورَيْنِ أُوتِيتَهُمَا لَمْ يُؤْتَهُمَا نَبِيٌّ قَبْلَكَ: فَاتِحَةُ الْكِتَابِ، وَخَوَاتِيمُ سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ، لَنْ تَقْرَأَ بِحَرْفٍ مِنْهُمَا إِلَّا أُعْطِيتَه". رَوَاه مُسْلِم (1/ 806)، والنَّسَائِي (2/ 912). وعَنْ أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «مَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنْجِيلِ مِثْلَ أُمِّ القُرْآنِ، وَهِيَ السَّبْعُ المَثَانِي". قَالَ اللهُ: «وَهِيَ مَقْسُومَةٌ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَ عَبْدِي، وَلِعَبْدِي مَا سَأَلَ". رَوَاه التِّرمِذِيُّ (5/ 3125) وصَحَّحَهُ، والنَّسَائِي (2/ 914). عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ قَالَ: كُنَّا فِي مَسِيرٍ لَنَا، فَنَزَلْنَا، فَجَاءَتْ جَارِيَةٌ فَقَالَتْ: إِنَّ سَيِّدَ الْحَيِّ سَلِيمٌ، وَإِنَّ نَفَرَنَا غُيَّبٌ، فَهَلْ مِنْكُمْ رَاقٍ؟ فَقَامَ مَعَهَا رَجُلٌ مَا كُنَّا نَأْبُنُهُ بِرُقْيَةٍ، فَرَقَاهُ فَبَرَأَ، فَأَمَرَ لَهُ بِثَلاَثِينَ شَاةً وَسَقَانَا لَبَنًا فَلَمَّا رَجَعَ قُلْنَا لَهُ أَكُنْتَ تُحْسِنُ رُقْيَةً أَوْ كُنْتَ تَرْقِي قَالَ: مَا رَقَيْتُ إِلاَّ بِأُمِّ الْكِتَابِ قُلْنَا. لاَ تُحْدِثُوا شَيْئًا حَتَّى نَأْتِيَ أَوْ نَسْأَلَ النَّبِيَّ -صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ-، فَلَمَّا قَدِمْنَا الْمَدِينَةَ ذَكَرْنَاهُ لِلنَّبِيِّ -صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ- فَقَالَ: "وَمَا كَانَ يُدْرِيهِ أَنَّهَا رُقْيَةٌ؟ اقْسِمُوا وَاضْرِبُوا لِي بِسَهْمٍ". الأحاديث الضعيفة التي وردت فى فضل سورة الفاتحة هام جداً: تم وضع الأحاديث الضعيفة هنا لكي لايؤخذ بها و يعلم الناس أنها ضعيفة. * فاتحة الكتاب تجزي ما لا يجزي شيء من القرآن، ولو أن فاتحة الكتاب جعلت في كفة الميزان، وجعل القرآن في الكفة الأخرى، لفضلت فاتحة الكتاب على القرآن سبع مرات. [((ضعيف جدا))/سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة 3996]. * فاتحة الكتاب شفاء من السم. [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة 3997]. * إذا وضعت جنبك على الفراش وقرأت {فاتحة الكتاب} و {قل هو الله أحد} فقد أمنت من كل شيء إلا الموت. [ضعيف/ ضعيف الترغيب و الترهيب 347]. * فاتحة الكتاب وآية الكرسي لا يقرؤهما عبد في دار فيصيبهم ذلك اليوم عين إنس وجن. [ضعيف/ضعيف الجامع الصغير 3952]. * فاتحة الكتاب شفاء من كل داء. [(ضعيف) / ضعيف الجامع الصغير 3951]. * فاتحة الكتاب تعدل بثلثي القرآن. [(ضعيف) / ضعيف الجامع الصغير 3949].
text.tr: faḍlu suwrahi ālfaātiḥah ālaḥādyth ālṣḥyḥh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh ālfātḥh ʿan aabiy saʿiydi bni ālmuʿalaāā raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: qaāla liya ālnabbiyu ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma: aalāa auʿalimmuka aaʿẓama suwrahin fiy ālqurāni qabla aan aakhruja mina ālmasjidi". fadhahaba ālnabbiyu ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma liyakhruja mina ālmasjidi fadhakarrtuhu faqaāla: "(ālḥamdu lilahhi rabi ālʿaālamiyna). hiya ālsabbʿu ālmathaāniy waālqurānu ālʿaẓiymu āladhdhiy auwtiytuhu». rawaāh ālbukhaāriy (6/ 4474) waaḥmad (24/ 15730). wʿan ʿabdi āllhi bni ʿabaāāsin raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhumaā qaāla: baynamaā jibriylu qaāʿidun ʿinda ālnabbiyi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma samiʿa naqiyḍanā min fawqihi farafaʿa raasahu faqaāla: "hadhaā baābun mina ālsammaāʾi futiḥa ālyawma lam yuftaḥ qaṭu iilaāā ālyawma fanazala minhu malakun faqaāla: hadhaā malakun nazala iilaā ālaarḍi lam yanzil qaṭu iilaāā ālyawma fasalamma waqaāla: aabshir binuwrayni auwtiytahumaā lam yuutahumaā nabiyun qablaka: faātiḥahu ālkitaābi wakhawaātiymu suwrahi ālbaqarahi lan taqraaa biḥarfin minhumaā iilaāā auʿṭiytah". rawaāh muslim (1/ 806) wālnassaāiiy (2/ 912). wʿan aubayi bni kaʿbin raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: qaāla ālnabbiyu ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma: «maā aanzala āllahhu fiy āltawwraāhi waāliinjiyli mithla aumi ālqurāni wahiya ālsabbʿu ālmathaāniy". qaāla āllhu: «wahiya maqsuwmahun bayniy wabayna ʿabdiy waliʿabdiy maā saaala". rawaāh āltirrmidhiyu (5/ 3125) wṣaḥaḥḥahu wālnassaāiiy (2/ 914). ʿan aabiy saʿiydin ālkhudriyi qaāla: kunaāā fiy masiyrin lanaā fanazalnaā fajaāʾat jaāriyahun faqaālat: iina sayidda ālḥayi saliymun waiina nafaranaā ghuyabbun fahal minkum raāqin faqaāma maʿahaā rajulun maā kunaāā naabunuhu biruqyahin faraqaāhu fabaraaa faaamara lahu bithalāathiyna shaāhan wasaqaānaā labananā falamaāā rajaʿa qulnaā lahu aakunta tuḥsinu ruqyahan aaw kunta tarqiy qaāla: maā raqaytu iilāa biaumi ālkitaābi qulnaā. lāa tuḥdithuwā shayianā ḥataāā naatiya aaw nasaala ālnabbiya -ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma- falamaāā qadimnaā ālmadiynaha dhakarnaāhu lilnabbiyi -ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma- faqaāla: "wamaā kaāna yudriyhi aanahhaā ruqyahun āqsimuwā waāḍribuwā liy bisahmin". ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh ālfātḥh hām jdāan: tm wḍʿ ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh hnā lky lāyukhdh bhā w yʿlm ālnās anhā ḍʿyfh. * fātḥh ālktāb tjzy mā lā yjzy shyʾ mn ālqrān wlw an fātḥh ālktāb jʿlt fy kfh ālmyzān wjʿl ālqrān fy ālkfh ālakhrā lfḍlt fātḥh ālktāb ʿlā ālqrān sbʿ mrāt. [((ḍʿyf jdā))/slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh 3996]. * fātḥh ālktāb shfāʾ mn ālsm. [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh 3997]. * idhā wḍʿt jnbk ʿlā ālfrāsh wqrat {fātḥh ālktāb} w {ql hw āllh aḥd} fqd amnt mn kl shyʾ ilā ālmwt. [ḍʿyf/ ḍʿyf āltrghyb w āltrhyb 347]. * fātḥh ālktāb wāyh ālkrsy lā yqruhmā ʿbd fy dār fyṣybhm dhlk ālywm ʿyn ins wjn. [ḍʿyf/ḍʿyf āljāmʿ ālṣghyr 3952]. * fātḥh ālktāb shfāʾ mn kl dāʾ. [(ḍʿyf) / ḍʿyf āljāmʿ ālṣghyr 3951]. * fātḥh ālktāb tʿdl bthlthy ālqrān. [(ḍʿyf) / ḍʿyf āljāmʿ ālṣghyr 3949].
text.fr:
**Mérite de la sourate Al-Fatiha** **Hadiths authentiques rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Fatiha :** - D'après Abou Sa'id ibn Al-Mu'alla (qu'Allah l'agrée), il a dit : Le Prophète (paix et salut sur lui) me dit : "Ne t'enseignerai-je pas la plus grande sourate du Coran avant que je ne sorte de la mosquée ?" Le Prophète (paix et salut sur lui) se dirigea vers la sortie de la mosquée, alors je le lui rappelai. Il dit : "(Al-Hamdu lillahi rabbi l-'alamin - Louange à Allah, Seigneur des mondes). Ce sont les sept versets répétés (as-Sab' al-Mathani) et le Coran sublime qui m'a été donné." (Rapporté par Al-Bukhari et Ahmad). - D'après Abdullah ibn Abbas (qu'Allah les agrée), il a dit : Alors que Gabriel était assis auprès du Prophète (paix et salut sur lui), il entendit un bruit venant d'en haut. Il leva la tête et dit : "C'est une porte du ciel qui a été ouverte aujourd'hui, elle n'avait jamais été ouverte auparavant." Un ange en descendit. Il dit : "C'est un ange descendu sur terre, il n'était jamais descendu auparavant." L'ange salua et dit : "Réjouis-toi de deux lumières qui t'ont été données et qui n'ont été données à aucun prophète avant toi : la Fatiha du Livre et les versets de la fin de la sourate Al-Baqara. Tu ne liras pas une lettre de l'une d'elles sans qu'elle ne te soit accordée." (Rapporté par Muslim et An-Nasa'i). - D'après Ubayy ibn Ka'b (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Prophète (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Allah n'a rien révélé dans la Thora et l'Évangile de semblable à la Mère du Coran (Umm al-Qur'an). Ce sont les sept versets répétés (as-Sab' al-Mathani)." Et Allah dit : "Elle est divisée entre Moi et Mon serviteur, et Mon serviteur aura ce qu'il demande." (Rapporté par At-Tirmidhi qui l'a authentifié, et An-Nasa'i). - D'après Abou Sa'id Al-Khudri (qu'Allah l'agrée), il a dit : Nous étions en voyage, nous fîmes halte. Une servante vint dire : "Le maître du clan est malade (piqué), et nos hommes sont absents. Y a-t-il parmi vous quelqu'un qui pratique la ruqya (incantation licite) ?" Un homme se leva avec elle (nous ne le soupçonnions pas de connaître la ruqya). Il fit la ruqya pour lui et il fut guéri. L'homme ordonna qu'on lui donne trente brebis et nous donna du lait à boire. À son retour, nous lui dîmes : "Savais-tu pratiquer la ruqya ?" Il dit : "Je n'ai fait de ruqya qu'avec la Mère du Livre (Al-Fatiha)." Nous dîmes : "Ne faites rien avant d'arriver (auprès du Prophète) ou de demander au Prophète (paix et salut sur lui)." Quand nous arrivâmes à Médine, nous en parlâmes au Prophète (paix et salut sur lui), il dit : "Comment savait-il que c'est une ruqya ? Partagez (les brebis) et attribuez-moi une part." (Rapporté par Al-Bukhari et Muslim). **Hadiths faibles rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Fatiha :**
***Important : Ces hadiths faibles sont mentionnés afin qu'ils ne soient pas pris en compte et que les gens sachent qu'ils sont faibles.** *
- La Fatiha du Livre compense ce qu'aucune partie du Coran ne compense. Si on plaçait la Fatiha du Livre dans un plateau de la balance et le reste du Coran dans l'autre, la Fatiha l'emporterait sept fois. [Très faible / Silsila ad-Da'ifa 3996].
- La Fatiha du Livre est un antidote contre le poison. [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 3997].
- Si tu te couches sur le côté dans ton lit et que tu lis {Al-Fatiha} et {Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad}, tu seras préservé de tout, sauf de la mort. [Faible / Da'if At-Targhib wa At-Tarhib 347].
- Un serviteur ne lit pas Al-Fatiha et Ayat al-Kursî dans une maison sans que ce jour-là, aucun mauvais œil d'humain ou de djinn ne les atteigne. [Faible / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 3952].
- Al-Fatiha est un remède à toute maladie. [Faible / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 3951].
- Al-Fatiha équivaut aux deux tiers du Coran. [Faible / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 3949].
text.en:
**Virtue of Surah Al-Fatiha** **Authentic hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al-Fatiha:** - From Abu Sa'id ibn Al-Mu'alla (may Allah be pleased with him), he said: The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) said to me: "Shall I not teach you the greatest Surah in the Quran before you leave the mosque?" The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) headed towards the exit of the mosque, so I reminded him. He said: "(Al-Hamdu lillahi rabbi l-'alamin - Praise be to Allah, Lord of the worlds). It is the seven oft-repeated verses (as-Sab' al-Mathani) and the Great Quran that has been given to me." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Ahmad). - From Abdullah ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them), he said: While Jibril (Gabriel) was sitting with the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him), he heard a sound from above. He raised his head and said: "This is a gate in heaven opened today; it has never been opened before." An angel descended from it. He said: "This is an angel who has descended to earth; he has never descended before." The angel greeted and said: "Rejoice in two lights that have been given to you and were not given to any prophet before you: the Opening of the Book (Fatiha) and the concluding verses of Surah Al-Baqarah. You will never recite a letter of them without being given it." (Reported by Muslim and An-Nasa'i). - From Ubayy ibn Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him), the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Allah has not revealed in the Torah or the Gospel anything like the Mother of the Quran (Umm al-Qur'an). It is the seven oft-repeated verses (as-Sab' al-Mathani)." And Allah said: "It is divided between Me and My servant, and My servant will have what he asks for." (Reported by At-Tirmidhi who authenticated it, and An-Nasa'i). - From Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him), he said: We were on a journey, and we camped. A slave girl came and said: "The master of the clan has been stung (bitten), and our men are absent. Is there anyone among you who can perform ruqya (licit incantation)?" A man stood up with her (we did not suspect him of knowing ruqya). He performed ruqya for him and he was cured. The man ordered that he be given thirty sheep and gave us milk to drink. When he returned, we said to him: "Did you know how to perform ruqya?" He said: "I only performed ruqya with the Mother of the Book (Al-Fatiha)." We said: "Do not do anything until we come (to the Prophet) or ask the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him)." When we arrived in Madinah, we mentioned it to the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him). He said: "How did he know that it is a ruqya? Divide (the sheep) and set aside a share for me." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim). **Weak hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al-Fatiha:**
***Important: These weak hadiths are mentioned so that they are not taken into account and people know they are weak.** *
- The Opening of the Book suffices for what nothing of the Quran suffices. If the Opening of the Book were placed on one scale of the balance, and the Quran on the other, the Opening would outweigh the Quran seven times. [Very weak / Silsila ad-Da'ifa 3996].
- The Opening of the Book is a cure for poison. [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 3997].
- If you lie on your side in bed and recite {Al-Fatiha} and {Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad}, you will be safe from everything except death. [Weak / Da'if At-Targhib wa At-Tarhib 347].
- No servant recites Al-Fatiha and Ayat al-Kursi in a house without that day, no evil eye of human or jinn afflicting them. [Weak / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 3952].
- Al-Fatiha is a cure for every disease. [Weak / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 3951].
- Al-Fatiha equals two-thirds of the Quran. [Weak / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 3949].

notes.fr: Ceci est un texte explicatif sur le mérite des sourates, contenant des hadiths authentiques et une mise en garde contre les hadiths faibles.
notes.en: This is an explanatory text on the virtue of the surahs, containing authentic hadiths and a warning against weak hadiths.

text.ar: فَضْلُ سُورَةِ الْبَقَرَةِ الأحاديث الصحيحة التي وردت فى فضل سورة البقرة عَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ الْبَاهِلِيِّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يَقُولُ: "اقْرَءُوا سُورَةَ الْبَقَرَةِ، فَإِنَّ أَخْذَهَا بَرَكَةٌ، وَتَرْكَهَا حَسْرَةٌ، وَلَا تَسْتَطِيعُهَا الْبَطَلَةُ". رَوَاه مُسْلِم (1/ 804)، وأَحمَد فِي "المُسْنَد" (36/ 22146). وعَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَالَ: "إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ يَنْفِرُ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ الَّذِي تُقْرَأُ فِيهِ سُورَةُ الْبَقَرَةِ". رَوَاه مُسْلِم (1/ 780)، والتِّرمِذِي (5/ 2877). عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَالَ: .لَا تَجْعَلُوا بُيُوتَكُمْ مَقَابِرَ، إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ يَنْفِرُ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ الَّذِي تُقْرَأُ فِيهِ سُورَةُ الْبَقَرَةِ. رَوَاه مُسْلِم (780) عَنِ ‏ ‏النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ ‏ رَضِيَ الله عّنْهُ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ‏ ‏صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ‏ ‏قَالَ: ‏‏"إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَتَبَ كِتَابًا قَبْلَ أَنْ يَخْلُقَ السَّمَوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِأَلْفَيْ عَامٍ فَأَنْزَلَ مِنْهُ آيَتَيْنِ فَخَتَمَ بِهِمَا سُورَةَ ‏ ‏الْبَقَرَةِ ‏ ‏وَلَا يُقْرَآنِ فِي دَارٍ ثَلَاثَ لَيَالٍ فَيَقْرَبَهَا الشَّيْطَانُ". أحمد (4/274 ، رقم 18438) ، والترمذي (5/159 ، رقم 2882). عَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ الْبَاهِلِيِّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، يَقُولُ: "اقْرَءُوا الزَّهْرَاوَيْنِ: الْبَقَرَةَ وَسُورَةَ آلِ عِمْرَانَ، فَإِنَّهُمَا تَأْتِيَانِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ كَأَنَّهُمَا غَمَامَتَانِ، تُحَاجَّانِ عَنْ أَصْحَابِهِمَا". رَوَاه مُسْلِم (1/ 840)، وأَحمَد فِي "المُسْنَد" (36/ 22193). فَضْلُ آيَةِ الْكُرْسِيِّ عَنْ أُبَيِّ بْنِ كَعْبٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: "يَا أَبَا الْمُنْذِرِ، أَتَدْرِي أَيُّ آيَةٍ مِنْ كِتَابِ اللهِ مَعَكَ أَعْظَمُ؟" قَالَ: قُلْتُ: اللهُ وَرَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ. قَالَ: "يَا أَبَا الْمُنْذِرِ أَتَدْرِي أَيُّ آيَةٍ مِنْ كِتَابِ اللهِ مَعَكَ أَعْظَمُ؟" قَالَ: قُلْتُ: {اللهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ} [البَقَرَة: 255]. قَالَ: فَضَرَبَ فِي صَدْرِي، وَقَالَ: «وَاللهِ؛ لِيَهْنِكَ الْعِلْمُ أَبَا الْمُنْذِرِ". رَوَاه مُسْلِم (1/ 810)، وأَبُو دَاوُد (2/ 1460). وعَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ الْبَاهِلِيِّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: "مَنْ قَرَأَ آيَةَ الْكُرْسِيِّ فِي دُبُرِ كُلِّ صَلَاةٍ مَكْتُوبَةٍ، لَمْ يَمْنَعْهُ مِنْ دُخُولِ الْجَنَّةِ إِلَّا أَنْ يَمُوتَ". رَوَاه النَّسَائِيُّ فِي "السُّنَن الكُبْرَى" (9/ 9848)، والطَبَرَانِيُّ فِي "المُعجَم الكَبِير" (8/ 7523). فَضْلُ الْآيَتَيْنِ مِنْ آخِرِ سُورَةِ البَقَرَةِ عَنْ أَبِي مَسْعُودٍ الْأَنْصَارِيِّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: "مَنْ قَرَأَ بِالْآيَتَيْنِ مِنْ آخِرِ سُورَةِ البَقَرَةِ فِي لَيْلَةٍ، كَفَتَـاهُ". رَوَاه البُخَارِي (6/ 5008، 5009)، ومُسْلِم (1/ 808). الأحاديث الضعيفة التي وردت فى فضل سورة البقرة * آيتان هما قرآن، وهما يشفعان، وهما مما يحبهما الله، الآيتان في آخر سورة البقرة. [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/154]. * من قرأ سورة البقرة توج بتاج في الجنة.((موضوع)) [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/4633]. * "إن لكل شيء سناما، وإن سنام القرآن، سورة البقرة ، من قرأها في بيته ليلا لم يدخله الشيطان ثلاث ليال، ومن قرأها في بيته نهارا لم يدخله الشيطان ثلاثة أيام".
text.tr: faḍlu suwrahi ālbaqarahi ālaḥādyth ālṣḥyḥh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh ālbqrh ʿan aabiy aumaāmaha ālbaāhiliyi raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: samiʿtu rasuwla āllahhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma yaquwlu: "āqraʾuwā suwraha ālbaqarahi faiina aakhdhahaā barakahun watarkahaā ḥasrahun walaā tastaṭiyʿuhaā ālbaṭalahu". rawaāh muslim (1/ 804) waaḥmad fiy "ālmusnad" (36/ 22146). wʿan aabiy hurayraha raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu aana rasuwla āllhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma qaāla: "iina ālshayyṭaāna yanfiru mina ālbayti āladhdhiy tuqraau fiyhi suwrahu ālbaqarahi". rawaāh muslim (1/ 780) wāltirrmidhiy (5/ 2877). ʿan aabiy hurayraha aana rasuwla āllhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma qaāla: .laā tajʿaluwā buyuwtakum maqaābira iina ālshayyṭaāna yanfiru mina ālbayti āladhdhiy tuqraau fiyhi suwrahu ālbaqarahi. rawaāh muslim (780) ʿani ‏ ‏ālnuʿʿmaāni bni bashiyrin ‏ raḍiya āllh ʿnnhu aana rasuwla āllahhi ‏ ‏ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma ‏ ‏qaāla: ‏‏"iina āllahha kataba kitaābanā qabla aan yakhluqa ālsammawaāti waālaarḍa biaalfay ʿaāmin faaanzala minhu āyatayni fakhatama bihimaā suwraha ‏ ‏ālbaqarahi ‏ ‏walaā yuqraāni fiy daārin thalaātha layaālin fayaqrabahaā ālshayyṭaānu". aḥmd (4/274 rqm 18438) wāltrmdhy (5/159 rqm 2882). ʿan aabiy aumaāmaha ālbaāhiliyi raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: samiʿtu rasuwla āllhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma yaquwlu: "āqraʾuwā ālzahhraāwayni: ālbaqaraha wasuwraha āli ʿimraāna faiinahhumaā taatiyaāni yawma ālqiyaāmahi kaaanahhumaā ghamaāmataāni tuḥaājaāāni ʿan aaṣḥaābihimaā". rawaāh muslim (1/ 840) waaḥmad fiy "ālmusnad" (36/ 22193). faḍlu āyahi ālkursiyi ʿan aubayi bni kaʿbin raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: qaāla rasuwlu āllhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma: "yaā aabaā ālmundhiri aatadriy aayu āyahin min kitaābi āllhi maʿaka aaʿẓamu" qaāla: qultu: āllhu warasuwluhu aaʿlamu. qaāla: "yaā aabaā ālmundhiri aatadriy aayu āyahin min kitaābi āllhi maʿaka aaʿẓamu" qaāla: qultu: {āllhu laā iilaha iilaāā huwa ālḥayu ālqayuwwmu} [ālbaqarah: 255]. qaāla: faḍaraba fiy ṣadriy waqaāla: «waāllhi liyahnika ālʿilmu aabaā ālmundhiri". rawaāh muslim (1/ 810) waabuw daāwud (2/ 1460). wʿan aabiy aumaāmaha ālbaāhiliyi raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: qaāla rasuwlu āllhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma: "man qaraaa āyaha ālkursiyi fiy duburi kuli ṣalaāhin maktuwbahin lam yamnaʿhu min dukhuwli āljanahhi iilaāā aan yamuwta". rawaāh ālnassaāiiyu fiy "ālsunnan ālkubraā" (9/ 9848) wālṭabaraāniyu fiy "ālmuʿjam ālkabiyr" (8/ 7523). faḍlu ālāyatayni min ākhiri suwrahi ālbaqarahi ʿan aabiy masʿuwdin ālaanṣaāriyi raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: qaāla ālnabbiyu ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma: "man qaraaa biālāyatayni min ākhiri suwrahi ālbaqarahi fiy laylahin kafataāhu". rawaāh ālbukhaāriy (6/ 5008 5009) wmuslim (1/ 808). ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh ālbqrh * āytān hmā qrān whmā yshfʿān whmā mmā yḥbhmā āllh ālāytān fy ākhr swrh ālbqrh. [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/154]. * mn qra swrh ālbqrh twj btāj fy āljnh.((mwḍwʿ)) [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/4633]. * "in lkl shyʾ snāmā win snām ālqrān swrh ālbqrh mn qrahā fy byth lylā lm ydkhlh ālshyṭān thlāth lyāl wmn qrahā fy byth nhārā lm ydkhlh ālshyṭān thlāthh ayām".
text.fr:
**Mérite de la sourate Al-Baqara** **Hadiths authentiques rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Baqara :** - D'après Abou Umama Al-Bahili (qu'Allah l'agrée), il a dit : J'ai entendu le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) dire : "Lisez la sourate Al-Baqara, car sa récitation est une bénédiction (baraka), son abandon est un regret, et les magiciens ne peuvent la supporter." (Rapporté par Muslim et Ahmad). - D'après Abou Hourayra (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Satan fuit la maison où l'on récite la sourate Al-Baqara." (Rapporté par Muslim et At-Tirmidhi). - D'après Abou Hourayra, que le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Ne faites pas de vos maisons des tombeaux. Satan fuit la maison où l'on récite la sourate Al-Baqara." (Rapporté par Muslim). - D'après An-Nu'man ibn Bashir (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Allah a écrit un Livre deux mille ans avant la création des cieux et de la terre. Il en a fait descendre deux versets par lesquels Il a conclu la sourate Al-Baqara. Ils ne sont pas lus dans une maison pendant trois nuits sans que Satan ne s'en approche." (Rapporté par Ahmad et At-Tirmidhi). - D'après Abou Umama Al-Bahili (qu'Allah l'agrée), il a dit : J'ai entendu le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) dire : "Lisez les deux fleurs (az-Zahrawain) : Al-Baqara et la famille d'Imran (Al 'Imran), car elles viendront le Jour de la Résurrection comme deux nuages (ou deux ombres) plaidant pour leurs compagnons." (Rapporté par Muslim et Ahmad). **Mérite d'Ayat al-Kursî (le Verset du Trône) :** - D'après Ubayy ibn Ka'b (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Ô Abou Al-Mundhir, sais-tu quel verset du Livre d'Allah que tu as avec toi est le plus grand ?" Je dis : "Allah et Son messager savent mieux." Il dit : "Ô Abou Al-Mundhir, sais-tu quel verset du Livre d'Allah que tu as avec toi est le plus grand ?" Je dis : "{Allah ! Il n'y a de divinité à part Lui, le Vivant, Celui qui subsiste par Lui-même (Al-Qayyum)} [Al-Baqara: 255]." Il dit alors me frappa la poitrine et dit : "Par Allah, puisses-tu être heureux dans ta science, Abou Al-Mundhir !" (Rapporté par Muslim et Abu Dawud). - D'après Abou Umama Al-Bahili (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Quiconque récite Ayat al-Kursî après chaque prière prescrite, seule la mort l'empêchera d'entrer au Paradis." (Rapporté par An-Nasa'i dans As-Sunan al-Kubra et At-Tabarani dans Al-Mu'jam al-Kabir). **Mérite des deux derniers versets de la sourate Al-Baqara :** - D'après Abou Mas'ud Al-Ansari (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Prophète (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Quiconque récite les deux derniers versets de la sourate Al-Baqara dans la nuit, ils lui suffiront." (Rapporté par Al-Bukhari et Muslim). **Hadiths faibles rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Baqara :** - Deux versets sont le Coran, ils intercèdent et font partie de ce qu'Allah aime : les deux versets à la fin de la sourate Al-Baqara. [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 154].
- Quiconque lit la sourate Al-Baqara sera ceint d'une couronne au Paradis. (Fabriqué) [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 4633].
- "Toute chose a une bosse (sommet), et la bosse du Coran est la sourate Al-Baqara. Quiconque la lit dans sa maison la nuit, Satan n'y entrera pas pendant trois nuits. Quiconque la lit dans sa maison le jour, Satan n'y entrera pas pendant trois jours."
text.en:
**Virtue of Surah Al-Baqarah** **Authentic hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al-Baqarah:** - From Abu Umamah Al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him), he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) say: "Recite Surah Al-Baqarah, for taking it is a blessing, and abandoning it is a regret, and the magicians cannot withstand it." (Reported by Muslim and Ahmad). - From Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Satan flees from the house in which Surah Al-Baqarah is recited." (Reported by Muslim and At-Tirmidhi). - From Abu Hurairah, that the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Do not make your houses like graves. Indeed, Satan flees from the house in which Surah Al-Baqarah is recited." (Reported by Muslim). - From An-Nu'man ibn Bashir (may Allah be pleased with him), the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Allah wrote a Book two thousand years before creating the heavens and the earth. He sent down from it two verses with which He concluded Surah Al-Baqarah. They are not recited in a house for three nights without Satan approaching it." (Reported by Ahmad and At-Tirmidhi). - From Abu Umamah Al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him), he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) say: "Recite the two bright ones (az-Zahrawain): Al-Baqarah and Al 'Imran, for they will come on the Day of Resurrection like two clouds (or two shades) pleading for their companions." (Reported by Muslim and Ahmad). **Virtue of Ayat al-Kursi (the Throne Verse):** - From Ubayy ibn Ka'b (may Allah be pleased with him), the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "O Abu Al-Mundhir, do you know which verse from the Book of Allah that you have is the greatest?" I said: "Allah and His Messenger know best." He said: "O Abu Al-Mundhir, do you know which verse from the Book of Allah that you have is the greatest?" I said: "{Allah - there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer of existence} [Al-Baqarah: 255]." He then struck me on the chest and said: "By Allah, may knowledge delight you, Abu Al-Mundhir!" (Reported by Muslim and Abu Dawud). - From Abu Umamah Al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him), the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi after every prescribed prayer, nothing prevents him from entering Paradise except death." (Reported by An-Nasa'i in As-Sunan al-Kubra and At-Tabarani in Al-Mu'jam al-Kabir). **Virtue of the last two verses of Surah Al-Baqarah:** - From Abu Mas'ud Al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him), the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Whoever recites the last two verses of Surah Al-Baqarah at night, they will suffice him." (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Muslim). **Weak hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al-Baqarah:** - Two verses are the Quran, they intercede, and they are among what Allah loves: the two verses at the end of Surah Al-Baqarah. [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 154].
- Whoever recites Surah Al-Baqarah will be crowned with a crown in Paradise. (Fabricated) [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 4633].
- "Everything has a hump (peak), and the hump of the Quran is Surah Al-Baqarah. Whoever recites it in his house at night, Satan will not enter it for three nights. Whoever recites it in his house during the day, Satan will not enter it for three days."

notes.fr: Ceci est un texte explicatif sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Baqarah, incluant ses versets majeurs, avec des hadiths authentiques et une mise en garde contre les hadiths faibles.
notes.en: This is an explanatory text on the virtue of Surah Al-Baqarah, including its major verses, with authentic hadiths and a warning against weak hadiths.

text.ar: فَضْلُ سُورَةِ آلِ عِمْرَانَ الأحاديث الصحيحة التي وردت فى فضل سورة آلِ عِمْرَان عَنْ أَبِي أُمَامَةَ الْبَاهِلِيِّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ رَسُولَ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، يَقُولُ: "اقْرَءُوا الزَّهْرَاوَيْنِ: الْبَقَرَةَ وَسُورَةَ آلِ عِمْرَانَ، فَإِنَّهُمَا تَأْتِيَانِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ كَأَنَّهُمَا غَمَامَتَانِ، تُحَاجَّانِ عَنْ أَصْحَابِهِمَا". رَوَاه مُسْلِم (1/ 840)، وأَحمَد فِي "المُسْنَد" (36/ 22193). الأحاديث الضعيفة التي وردت فى فضل سورة آلِ عِمْرَان * "من قرأ السورة التي يذكر فيها آل عمران يوم الجمعة ، صلى الله عليه و ملائكته حتى تجب الشمس". (موضوع) [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/ 415]. * اسم الله الأعظم الذي إذا دعي به أجاب في هذه الآية من آل عمران: "قل اللهم مالك الملك تؤتي الملك من تشاء" إلى آخره. [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة /2772]. * الشرك أخفى في أمتي من دبيب النمل على الصفا في الليلة الظلم اء، وأدناه أن تحب على شيء من الجور أو تبغض على شيء من العدل، وهل الدين إلا الحب في الله والبغض في الله؟ قال الله -تعالى-: {قل إن كنتم تحبون الله فاتبعوني يحببكم الله} (آل عمران: 31). [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/3755]. * من قرأ آخر "آل عمران في ليلة كتب له قيام ليلة". [ضعيف مشكاة المصابيح 2112]. * "من قرأ سورة آل عمران يوم الجمعة صلت عليه الملائكة إلى الليل". [ضعيف مشكاة المصابيح 2113]. * ما من رجل يكون على دابة صعبة فيقول في أذنها: "أفغير دين الله يبغون وله أسلم من في السموات والأرض طوعاً وكرهاً وإليه يرجعون" (آل عمران: 83) إلا وقفت لإذن الله تعالى. [ضعيف الكلم الطيب 177]. * ما خيب الله تعالى عبدا قام في جوف الليل، فافتتح سورة (البقرة) و(آل عمران)، ونعم كنز المرء (البقرة) و(آل عمران). [ضعيف الجامع الصغير 5063].
text.tr: faḍlu suwrahi āli ʿimraāna ālaḥādyth ālṣḥyḥh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh āli ʿimraān ʿan aabiy aumaāmaha ālbaāhiliyi raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: samiʿtu rasuwla āllhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma yaquwlu: "āqraʾuwā ālzahhraāwayni: ālbaqaraha wasuwraha āli ʿimraāna faiinahhumaā taatiyaāni yawma ālqiyaāmahi kaaanahhumaā ghamaāmataāni tuḥaājaāāni ʿan aaṣḥaābihimaā". rawaāh muslim (1/ 840) waaḥmad fiy "ālmusnad" (36/ 22193). ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh āli ʿimraān * "mn qra ālswrh ālty ydhkr fyhā āl ʿmrān ywm āljmʿh ṣlā āllh ʿlyh w mlāikth ḥtā tjb ālshms". (mwḍwʿ) [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/ 415]. * āsm āllh ālaʿẓm āldhy idhā dʿy bh ajāb fy hdhh ālāyh mn āl ʿmrān: "ql āllhm mālk ālmlk tuty ālmlk mn tshāʾ" ilā ākhrh. [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh /2772]. * ālshrk akhfā fy amty mn dbyb ālnml ʿlā ālṣfā fy āllylh ālẓlm āʾ wadnāh an tḥb ʿlā shyʾ mn āljwr aw tbghḍ ʿlā shyʾ mn ālʿdl whl āldyn ilā ālḥb fy āllh wālbghḍ fy āllh qāl āllh -tʿālā-: {ql in kntm tḥbwn āllh fātbʿwny yḥbbkm āllh} (āl ʿmrān: 31). [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/3755]. * mn qra ākhr "āl ʿmrān fy lylh ktb lh qyām lylh". [ḍʿyf mshkāh ālmṣābyḥ 2112]. * "mn qra swrh āl ʿmrān ywm āljmʿh ṣlt ʿlyh ālmlāikh ilā āllyl". [ḍʿyf mshkāh ālmṣābyḥ 2113]. * mā mn rjl ykwn ʿlā dābh ṣʿbh fyqwl fy adhnhā: "afghyr dyn āllh ybghwn wlh aslm mn fy ālsmwāt wālarḍ ṭwʿāan wkrhāan wilyh yrjʿwn" (āl ʿmrān: 83) ilā wqft lidhn āllh tʿālā. [ḍʿyf ālklm ālṭyb 177]. * mā khyb āllh tʿālā ʿbdā qām fy jwf āllyl fāfttḥ swrh (ālbqrh) w(āl ʿmrān) wnʿm knz ālmrʾ (ālbqrh) w(āl ʿmrān). [ḍʿyf āljāmʿ ālṣghyr 5063].
text.fr:
**Mérite de la sourate Al 'Imran (La famille d'Imran)** **Hadiths authentiques rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al 'Imran :** - D'après Abou Umama Al-Bahili (qu'Allah l'agrée), il a dit : J'ai entendu le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) dire : "Lisez les deux fleurs (az-Zahrawain) : Al-Baqara et la famille d'Imran (Al 'Imran), car elles viendront le Jour de la Résurrection comme deux nuages (ou deux ombres) plaidant pour leurs compagnons." (Rapporté par Muslim et Ahmad). **Hadiths faibles rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al 'Imran :** - "Quiconque lit la sourate dans laquelle la famille d'Imran est mentionnée le vendredi, Allah et Ses anges prient sur lui jusqu'au coucher du soleil." (Fabriqué) [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 415].
- Le nom suprême d'Allah (ism Allah al-a'dham) par lequel il exauce se trouve dans ce verset d'Al 'Imran : "Dis : 'Ô Allah, Maître du royaume, Tu donnes la royauté à qui Tu veux, et Tu arraches la royauté à qui Tu veux...'" jusqu'à la fin. [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 2772].
- L'association (shirk) est plus cachée dans ma communauté que la marche des fourmis sur une roche lisse par une nuit obscure. Son niveau le plus bas est d'aimer quelque chose d'injuste ou de détester quelque chose de juste. La religion n'est-elle pas l'amour pour Allah et la haine pour Allah ? Allah, le Très-Haut, dit : {Dis : "Si vous aimez vraiment Allah, suivez-moi, Allah vous aimera alors et vous pardonnera vos péchés."} (Al 'Imran: 31). [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 3755].
- Quiconque lit la fin d'Al 'Imran dans la nuit, il lui sera compté comme une nuit de prière surérogatoire (qiyam layl). [Faible / Mishkat al-Masabih 2112].
- "Quiconque lit la sourate Al 'Imran le vendredi, les anges prient sur lui jusqu'à la nuit." [Faible / Mishkat al-Masabih 2113].
- Il n'est pas un homme sur une monture rétive qui dise dans son oreille : "Est-ce une autre religion que celle d'Allah qu'ils recherchent, alors que se soumet à Lui, bon gré mal gré, tout ce qui est dans les cieux et la terre ? Et c'est vers Lui qu'ils seront ramenés." (Al 'Imran: 83) sans que la monture ne s'arrête par la permission d'Allah. [Faible / Al-Kalim at-Tayyib 177].
- Allah n'a jamais déçu un serviteur qui s'est levé au milieu de la nuit et a commencé par lire Al-Baqara et Al 'Imran. Quelle excellente richesse que Al-Baqara et Al 'Imran pour un homme. [Faible / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 5063].
text.en:
**Virtue of Surah Al 'Imran (The Family of Imran)** **Authentic hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al 'Imran:** - From Abu Umamah Al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him), he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) say: "Recite the two bright ones (az-Zahrawain): Al-Baqarah and Al 'Imran, for they will come on the Day of Resurrection like two clouds (or two shades) pleading for their companions." (Reported by Muslim and Ahmad). **Weak hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al 'Imran:** - "Whoever recites the surah in which the family of 'Imran is mentioned on Friday, Allah and His angels send blessings upon him until the sun sets." (Fabricated) [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 415].
- The greatest name of Allah (ism Allah al-a'dham) by which He answers is in this verse of Al 'Imran: "Say: 'O Allah, Master of the Kingdom, You give sovereignty to whom You will and You take sovereignty away from whom You will...'" to the end. [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 2772].
- Polytheism (shirk) is more hidden in my nation than the crawling of ants on a smooth rock in a dark night. Its lowest level is to love something of injustice or to hate something of justice. Is religion anything other than love for Allah and hatred for Allah? Allah, the Most High, says: {Say, [O Muhammad], "If you should love Allah, then follow me, [so] Allah will love you and forgive you your sins."} (Al 'Imran: 31). [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 3755].
- Whoever recites the end of Al 'Imran at night, it will be recorded for him as a night of voluntary prayer (qiyam layl). [Weak / Mishkat al-Masabih 2112].
- "Whoever recites Surah Al 'Imran on Friday, the angels send blessings upon him until nightfall." [Weak / Mishkat al-Masabih 2113].
- There is no man on a stubborn mount who says in its ear: "Is it other than the religion of Allah they desire, while to Him have submitted [all] those within the heavens and earth, willingly or by compulsion, and to Him they will be returned?" (Al 'Imran: 83) except that the mount will stop by Allah's permission. [Weak / Al-Kalim at-Tayyib 177].
- Allah has never disappointed a servant who stood in the middle of the night and began by reciting Al-Baqarah and Al 'Imran. What an excellent treasure Al-Baqarah and Al 'Imran are for a man. [Weak / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 5063].

notes.fr: Ceci est un texte explicatif sur le mérite de la sourate Al 'Imran, avec des hadiths authentiques et une mise en garde contre les hadiths faibles.
notes.en: This is an explanatory text on the virtue of Surah Al 'Imran, with authentic hadiths and a warning against weak hadiths.

text.ar: فَضْلُ سُورَةِ الكَهْفِ الأحاديث الصحيحة التي وردت فى فضل سورة الكَهْفِ عَنْ أَبِي الدَّرْدَاءِ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: "مَنْ حَفِظَ عَشْرَ آيَاتٍ مِنْ أَوَّلِ الْكَهْفِ، عُصِمَ مِنْ فِتْنَةِ الدَّجَّالِ". رَوَاه مُسْلِم (1/ 809)، وأَبُو دَاوُد (4/ 4323)، والتِّرمِذِي (5/ 2886). عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ سُوَرَةَ الْكَهْفِ فِي يَوْمِ الْجُمُعَةِ أَضَاءَ لَهُ مِنَ النُّورِ مَا بَيْنَ الْجُمُعَتَيْنِ. [صححه الألباني في صحيح الترغيب والترهيب/736]. عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ الْخُدْرِيِّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ سُورَةَ الْكَهْفِ لَيْلَةَ الْجُمُعَةِ أَضَاءَ لَهُ مِنَ النُّورِ فِيمَا بَيْنَهُ وَبَيْنَ الْبَيْتِ الْعَتِيق. [صححه الألباني في صحيح الترغيب والترهيب/736]. الأحاديث الضعيفة التي وردت فى فضل سورة الكَهْفِ * ألا أخبركم بسورة ملأت عظمتها ما بين السماء و الأرض؟ و لقارئها من الأجر مثل ذلك، و من قرأها غفر له ما بينه و بين الجمعة الأخرى، وزيادة ثلاثة أيام؟ قالوا: بلى قال: سورة الكهف. [ضعيف جداَ/ضعيف الجامع الصغير/ 2160]. * سورة الكهف تدعى في التوراة: الحائلة تحول بين قارئها وبين النار. [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/ 3259]. * من قرأ ثلاث آيات من أول الكهف عصم من فتنة الدجال. [ضعيف مشكاة المصابيح/ 2088].
text.tr: faḍlu suwrahi ālkahfi ālaḥādyth ālṣḥyḥh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh ālkahfi ʿan aabiy āldarrdaāʾi raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu ʿani ālnabbiyi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma qaāla: "man ḥafiẓa ʿashra āyaātin min aawalli ālkahfi ʿuṣima min fitnahi āldajjaāāli". rawaāh muslim (1/ 809) waabuw daāwud (4/ 4323) wāltirrmidhiy (5/ 2886). ʿan aabiy saʿiydin ālkhudriyi raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu aana ālnabbiya qaāla: man qaraaa suwaraha ālkahfi fiy yawmi āljumuʿahi aaḍaāʾa lahu mina ālnuwwri maā bayna āljumuʿatayni. [ṣḥḥh ālalbāny fy ṣḥyḥ āltrghyb wāltrhyb/736]. ʿan aabiy saʿiydin ālkhudriyi raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu aana ālnabbiya qaāla: man qaraaa suwraha ālkahfi laylaha āljumuʿahi aaḍaāʾa lahu mina ālnuwwri fiymaā baynahu wabayna ālbayti ālʿatiyq. [ṣḥḥh ālalbāny fy ṣḥyḥ āltrghyb wāltrhyb/736]. ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh ālkahfi * alā akhbrkm bswrh mlat ʿẓmthā mā byn ālsmāʾ w ālarḍ w lqārihā mn ālajr mthl dhlk w mn qrahā ghfr lh mā bynh w byn āljmʿh ālakhrā wzyādh thlāthh ayām qālwā: blā qāl: swrh ālkhf. [ḍʿyf jdāa/ḍʿyf āljāmʿ ālṣghyr/ 2160]. * swrh ālkhf tdʿā fy āltwrāh: ālḥāilh tḥwl byn qārihā wbyn ālnār. [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/ 3259]. * mn qra thlāth āyāt mn awl ālkhf ʿṣm mn ftnh āldjāl. [ḍʿyf mshkāh ālmṣābyḥ/ 2088].
text.fr:
**Mérite de la sourate Al-Kahf (La Caverne)** **Hadiths authentiques rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Kahf :** - D'après Abou Ad-Darda' (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Prophète (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Quiconque mémorise les dix premiers versets de la sourate Al-Kahf sera préservé de la tentation du Dajjal (l'Antéchrist)." (Rapporté par Muslim, Abu Dawud et At-Tirmidhi). - D'après Abou Sa'id Al-Khudri (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Prophète (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Quiconque lit la sourate Al-Kahf le vendredi, une lumière l'éclairera entre les deux vendredis." (Authentifié par Al-Albani dans Sahih At-Targhib wa At-Tarhib). - D'après Abou Sa'id Al-Khudri (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Prophète (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Quiconque lit la sourate Al-Kahf la nuit du vendredi, une lumière l'éclairera entre lui et la Maison antique (la Kaaba)." (Authentifié par Al-Albani dans Sahih At-Targhib wa At-Tarhib). **Hadiths faibles rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Kahf :** - "Ne vous informerais-je pas d'une sourate dont la grandeur remplit l'espace entre le ciel et la terre ? Son lecteur aura une récompense équivalente. Quiconque la lit, il lui sera pardonné entre ce vendredi et le suivant, avec trois jours supplémentaires." Ils dirent : "Si !" Il dit : "La sourate Al-Kahf." [Très faible / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 2160].
- "La sourate Al-Kahf est appelée dans la Thora : Al-Ha'ila (celle qui fait obstacle) car elle fait obstacle entre son lecteur et le Feu." [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 3259].
- "Quiconque lit trois versets du début d'Al-Kahf sera préservé de la tentation du Dajjal." [Faible / Mishkat al-Masabih 2088].
text.en:
**Virtue of Surah Al-Kahf (The Cave)** **Authentic hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al-Kahf:** - From Abu Ad-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him), the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Whoever memorizes the first ten verses of Surah Al-Kahf will be protected from the trial of the Dajjal (Antichrist)." (Reported by Muslim, Abu Dawud and At-Tirmidhi). - From Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him), the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Whoever reads Surah Al-Kahf on Friday, a light will shine for him between the two Fridays." (Authenticated by Al-Albani in Sahih At-Targhib wa At-Tarhib). - From Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him), the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "Whoever reads Surah Al-Kahf on the night of Friday, a light will shine for him between him and the Ancient House (the Kaaba)." (Authenticated by Al-Albani in Sahih At-Targhib wa At-Tarhib). **Weak hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al-Kahf:** - "Shall I not inform you of a surah whose greatness fills the space between the heaven and the earth? Its reciter will have a reward like that. Whoever recites it, he will be forgiven between this Friday and the next, plus three extra days." They said: "Yes!" He said: "Surah Al-Kahf." [Very weak / Da'if Al-Jami' as-Saghir 2160].
- "Surah Al-Kahf is called in the Torah: Al-Ha'ila (the Obstructing One) because it obstructs between its reciter and the Fire." [Silsila ad-Da'ifa 3259].
- "Whoever recites three verses from the beginning of Al-Kahf will be protected from the trial of the Dajjal." [Weak / Mishkat al-Masabih 2088].

notes.fr: Ceci est un texte explicatif sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Kahf, avec des hadiths authentiques et une mise en garde contre les hadiths faibles.
notes.en: This is an explanatory text on the virtue of Surah Al-Kahf, with authentic hadiths and a warning against weak hadiths.

text.ar: فَضْلُ سُورَةِ المُلْكِ الأحاديث الصحيحة التي وردت فى فضل سورة المُلْكِ عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: "سُورَةٌ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ ثَلَاثُونَ آيَةً، تَشْفَعُ لِصَاحِبِهَا حَتَّى يُغْفَرَ لَهُ: (تَبَارَكَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ الْمُلْك)". رَوَاه أَبُو دَاوُد (2/ 1400)، والتِّرمِذِي (5/ 2891)، وقَالَ: "حَدِيثٌ حَسَنٌ". عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: "كَانَ رَسُولُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَا يَنَامُ حَتَّى يَقْرَأَ الم تَنْزِيلُ السَّجْدَةَ، وَتَبَارَكَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ الْمُلْكُ". [رواه الترمذي وصححه الألباني /2316].
text.tr: faḍlu suwrahi ālmulki ālaḥādyth ālṣḥyḥh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh ālmulki ʿan aabiy hurayraha raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu ʿani ālnabbiyi ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma qaāla: "suwrahun mina ālqurāni thalaāthuwna āyahan tashfaʿu liṣaāḥibihaā ḥataāā yughfara lahu: (tabaāraka āladhdhiy biyadihi ālmulk)". rawaāh aabuw daāwud (2/ 1400) wāltirrmidhiy (5/ 2891) wqaāla: "ḥadiythun ḥasanun". ʿan jaābirin qaāla: "kaāna rasuwlu āllhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma laā yanaāmu ḥataāā yaqraaa ālm tanziylu ālsajjdaha watabaāraka āladhdhiy biyadihi ālmulku". [rwāh āltrmdhy wṣḥḥh ālalbāny /2316].
text.fr: **Mérite de la sourate Al-Mulk (La Royauté)** **Hadiths authentiques rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Mulk :** - D'après Abou Hourayra (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Prophète (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Il y a dans le Coran une sourate de trente versets qui intercède pour son compagnon jusqu'à ce qu'il soit pardonné : 'Béni soit Celui dans la main duquel est la royauté (Tabarak alladhi bi yadihi l-mulk).'" (Rapporté par Abu Dawud et At-Tirmidhi qui a dit : "Hadith bon (hasan)"). - D'après Jabir (qu'Allah l'agrée), il a dit : "Le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) ne s'endormait pas avant d'avoir lu 'Alif Lam Mim Tanzil As-Sajda' (sourate As-Sajda) et 'Tabarak alladhi bi yadihi l-mulk' (sourate Al-Mulk)." (Rapporté par At-Tirmidhi et authentifié par Al-Albani).
text.en: **Virtue of Surah Al-Mulk (The Sovereignty)** **Authentic hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al-Mulk:** - From Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him), the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) said: "There is a surah in the Quran of thirty verses which intercedes for its companion until he is forgiven: 'Blessed is He in whose hand is the sovereignty (Tabarak alladhi bi yadihi l-mulk).'" (Reported by Abu Dawud and At-Tirmidhi who said: "Good hadith (hasan)"). - From Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him), he said: "The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings be upon him) would not sleep until he had recited 'Alif Lam Mim Tanzil As-Sajda' (Surah As-Sajda) and 'Tabarak alladhi bi yadihi l-mulk' (Surah Al-Mulk)." (Reported by At-Tirmidhi and authenticated by Al-Albani).

notes.fr: Ceci est un texte explicatif sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Mulk, avec des hadiths authentiques.
notes.en: This is an explanatory text on the virtue of Surah Al-Mulk, with authentic hadiths.

text.ar: فَضْلُ سُورَةِ الكَافِرُون الأحاديث الصحيحة التي وردت فى فضل سورة الكَافِرُون عَنْ نَوْفَلٍ الأَشجَعِيُّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، أَنَّهُ أَتَى النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ عَلِّمْنِي شَيْئًا أَقُولُهُ إِذَا أَوَيْتُ إِلَى فِرَاشِي، قَالَ: "اقْرَأْ: (قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا الكَافِرُونَ) فَإِنَّهَا بَرَاءَةٌ مِنَ الشِّرْكِ". رَوَاه أَبُو دَاوُد (4/ 5055)، والتِّرمِذِي (5/ 3403) وصَحَّحَهُ.
text.tr: faḍlu suwrahi ālkaāfiruwn ālaḥādyth ālṣḥyḥh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh ālkaāfiruwn ʿan nawfalin ālaashjaʿiyu raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu aanahhu aataā ālnabbiya ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma faqaāla: yaā rasuwla āllahhi ʿalimmniy shayianā aaquwluhu iidhaā aawaytu iilaā firaāshiy qaāla: "āqraa: (qul yaā aayuhhaā ālkaāfiruwna) faiinahhaā baraāʾahun mina ālshirrki". rawaāh aabuw daāwud (4/ 5055) wāltirrmidhiy (5/ 3403) wṣaḥaḥḥahu.
text.fr: **Mérite de la sourate Al-Kafirun (Les Infidèles)** **Hadiths authentiques rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Kafirun :** - D'après Nawfal Al-Ashja'i (qu'Allah l'agrée), il vint trouver le Prophète (paix et salut sur lui) et dit : "Ô Messager d'Allah, enseigne-moi quelque chose que je puisse dire quand je me couche." Il dit : "Lis : 'Dis : Ô vous les infidèles (Qul ya ayyuha l-kafirun)', car c'est une déclaration d'innocence vis-à-vis du polythéisme (shirk)." (Rapporté par Abu Dawud et At-Tirmidhi qui l'a authentifié).
text.en: **Virtue of Surah Al-Kafirun (The Disbelievers)** **Authentic hadiths reported on the virtue of Surah Al-Kafirun:** - From Nawfal Al-Ashja'i (may Allah be pleased with him), he came to the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) and said: "O Messenger of Allah, teach me something to say when I go to my bed." He said: "Recite: 'Say: O you disbelievers (Qul ya ayyuha l-kafirun)', for it is a declaration of innocence from polytheism (shirk)." (Reported by Abu Dawud and At-Tirmidhi who authenticated it).

notes.fr: Ceci est un texte explicatif sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Kafirun, avec des hadiths authentiques.
notes.en: This is an explanatory text on the virtue of Surah Al-Kafirun, with authentic hadiths.

text.ar: فَضْلُ سُورَةِ الإِخْلاَصِ الأحاديث الصحيحة التي وردت فى فضل سورة الإِخْلاَصِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مَسْعُودٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: "أَيَعْجِزُ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَقْرَأَ كُلَّ لَيْلَةٍ ثُلُثَ الْقُرْآنِ؟" قَالُوا: وَمَنْ يُطِيقُ ذَلِكَ؟ قَالَ: "بَلَى؛ (قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ)". رَوَاه البُخَارِي (6/ 5015)، ومُسْلِم (1/ 811)، وأَحمَد (14/ 11053). وعَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ، قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ: إِنِّي أُحِبُّ هَذِهِ السُّورَةَ: (قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ). فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: "حُبُّكَ إِيَّاهَا أَدْخَلَكَ الْجَنَّةَ". رَوَاه البُخَارِي (1/ ص155) تَعلِيقاً، والتِّرمِذِي (5/ 2901) وحَسَّنَهُ. عن مُعَاذِ بْنِ أَنَسٍ الْجُهَنِيِّ رضي الله عنه عَنْ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: مَنْ قَرَأَ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ حَتَّى يَخْتِمَهَا عَشْرَ مَرَّاتٍ بَنَى اللَّهُ لَهُ قَصْرًا فِي الْجَنَّةِ ، فَقَالَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ : إِذَنْ أَسْتَكْثِرَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ : اللَّهُ أَكْثَرُ وَأَطْيَبُ. رواه أحمد (15057) ، وصححه الألباني في صحيح الجامع (6472). عَنْ مُعَاذِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ خُبَيْبٍ ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ ، قَالَ: خَرَجْنَا فِي لَيْلَةٍ مَطِيرَةٍ ، وَظُلْمَةٍ شَدِيدَةٍ ، نَطْلُبُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ يُصَلِّي لَنَا ، قَالَ : فَأَدْرَكْتُهُ ، فَقَالَ : قُلْ : فَلَمْ أَقُلْ شَيْئًا ، ثُمَّ قَالَ : قُلْ : فَلَمْ أَقُلْ شَيْئًا ، قَالَ : قُلْ ، فَقُلْتُ : مَا أَقُولُ ؟ قَالَ : قُلْ : " قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ , وَالْمُعَوِّذَتَيْنِ حِينَ تُمْسِي وَتُصْبِحُ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ تَكْفِيكَ مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ". [رواه الترمذي وصححه الألباني /2829]. عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ رضي الله عنه قَالَ: أَقْبَلْتُ مَعَ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَسَمِعَ رَجُلاً يَقْرَأُ "قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ ، اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ" ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ : وَجَبَتْ قُلْتُ : وَمَا وَجَبَتْ ؟ قَالَ : الْجَنَّةُ. [رواه الترمذي وصحح الألباني /2320]. عَنْ عُقْبَةَ بْنِ عَامِرٍ قَالَ لَقِيتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ لِي: يَا عُقْبَةُ بْنَ عَامِرٍ أَلا أُعَلِّمُكَ سُوَرًا مَا أُنْزِلَتْ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَلا فِي الزَّبُورِ و لا فِي الإِنْجِيلِ وَلا فِي الْفُرْقَانِ مِثْلُهُنَّ لا يَأْتِيَنَّ عَلَيْكَ لَيْلَةٌ إِلا قَرَأْتَهُنَّ فِيهَا قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ وَقُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الْفَلَقِ وَقُلْ أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ قَالَ عُقْبَةُ فَمَا أَتَتْ عَلَيَّ لَيْلَةٌ إِلا قَرَأْتُهُنَّ فِيهَا وَحُقَّ لِي أَنْ لا أَدَعَهُنَّ وَقَدْ أَمَرَنِي بِهِنَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ. [سلسلة الأحاديث الصحيحة/2861] الأحاديث الضعيفة التي وردت فى فضل سورة الإِخْلاَصِ * "من قرأ قل هو الله أحد في مرضه الذي يموت فيه، لم يفتن في قبره، وأمن من ضغطة القبر، و حملته الملائكة يوم القيامة بأكفها حتى تجيزه من الصراط إلى الجنة". (موضوع) [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/301]. * "من قرأ (قل هو الله أحد) عشرين مرة بنى الله له قصرا في الجنة". [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/1351]. * "من قرأ {قل هو الله أحد} مائتي مرة، غفرت له ذنوب مائتي سنة". [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/295]. * "من قرأ قل هو الله أحد مائتي مرة كتب الله له ألفا و خمسمائة حسنة، إلا أن يكون عليه دين". [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/300]. * "من مر بالمقابر فقرأ (قل هو الله أحد) إحدى عشرة مرة، ثم وهب أجره للأموات، أعطي من الأجر بعدد الأموات". [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/1290]. * "من صلى الصبح ثم قرأ (قل هو الله أحد) مائة مرة قبل أن يتكلم، فكلما قرأ (قل هو الله أحد) غفر له ذنب سنة". [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة/405]. * "من مر على المقابر فقرأ فيها إحدى عشرة مرة: {قل هو الله أحد} ثم وهب أجره للأموات أعطي من الأجر بعدد الأموات". [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة /3277]. * "من قرأ بعد صلاة الجمعة {قل هو الله أحد} و{قل أعوذ برب الفلق} و{قل أعوذ برب الناس} سبع مرات أجاره الله بها من السوء إلى الجمعة الأخرى". [سلسلة الأحاديث الضعيفة /4129].
text.tr: faḍlu suwrahi āliikhlāaṣi ālaḥādyth ālṣḥyḥh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh āliikhlāaṣi ʿan ʿabdi āllahhi bni masʿuwdin raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: qaāla rasuwlu āllahhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma: "aayaʿjizu aaḥadukum aan yaqraaa kula laylahin thulutha ālqurāni" qaāluwā: waman yuṭiyqu dhalika qaāla: "balaā (qul huwa āllahhu aaḥadun)". rawaāh ālbukhaāriy (6/ 5015) wmuslim (1/ 811) waaḥmad (14/ 11053). wʿan aanasi bni maālikin raḍiya āllahhu ʿanhu qaāla: jaāʾa rajulun iilaā rasuwli āllhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma faqaāla: iiniyy auḥibu hadhihi ālsuwwraha: (qul huwa āllahhu aaḥadun). faqaāla rasuwlu āllhi ṣalaāā āllhu ʿalayhi wasalamma: "ḥubukka iiyaāāhaā aadkhalaka āljanahha". rawaāh ālbukhaāriy (1/ ṣ155) taʿliyqāan wāltirrmidhiy (5/ 2901) wḥasannahu. ʿn muʿaādhi bni aanasin āljuhaniyi rḍy āllh ʿnh ʿan ālnabbiyi ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma qaāla: man qaraaa qul huwa āllahhu aaḥadun ḥataāā yakhtimahaā ʿashra maraāātin banaā āllahhu lahu qaṣranā fiy āljanahhi faqaāla ʿumaru bnu ālkhaṭaāābi : iidhan aastakthira yaā rasuwla āllahhi faqaāla rasuwlu āllahhi ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma : āllahhu aaktharu waaaṭyabu. rwāh aḥmd (15057) wṣḥḥh ālalbāny fy ṣḥyḥ āljāmʿ (6472). ʿan muʿaādhi bni ʿabdi āllahhi bni khubaybin ʿan aabiyhi qaāla: kharajnaā fiy laylahin maṭiyrahin waẓulmahin shadiydahin naṭlubu rasuwla āllahhi ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma yuṣaliyy lanaā qaāla : faaadraktuhu faqaāla : qul : falam aaqul shayianā thuma qaāla : qul : falam aaqul shayianā qaāla : qul faqultu : maā aaquwlu qaāla : qul : " qul huwa āllahhu aaḥadun , waālmuʿawidhdhatayni ḥiyna tumsiy watuṣbiḥu thalaātha maraāātin takfiyka min kuli shayʾin". [rwāh āltrmdhy wṣḥḥh ālalbāny /2829]. ʿan aabiy hurayraha rḍy āllh ʿnh qaāla: aaqbaltu maʿa ālnabbiyi ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma fasamiʿa rajulāan yaqraau "qul huwa āllahhu aaḥadun āllahhu ālṣammadu" faqaāla rasuwlu āllahhi ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma : wajabat qultu : wamaā wajabat qaāla : āljanahhu. [rwāh āltrmdhy wṣḥḥ ālalbāny /2320]. ʿan ʿuqbaha bni ʿaāmirin qaāla laqiytu rasuwla āllahhi ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma faqaāla liy: yaā ʿuqbahu bna ʿaāmirin aalā auʿalimmuka suwaranā maā aunzilat fiy āltawwraāhi walā fiy ālzabbuwri w lā fiy āliinjiyli walā fiy ālfurqaāni mithluhuna lā yaatiyana ʿalayka laylahun iilā qaraatahuna fiyhaā qul huwa āllahhu aaḥadun waqul aaʿuwdhu birabi ālfalaqi waqul aaʿuwdhu birabi ālnaāāsi qaāla ʿuqbahu famaā aatat ʿalaya laylahun iilā qaraatuhuna fiyhaā waḥuqa liy aan lā aadaʿahuna waqad aamaraniy bihina rasuwlu āllahhi ṣalaāā āllahhu ʿalayhi wasalamma. [slslh ālaḥādyth ālṣḥyḥh/2861] ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh ālty wrdt fā fḍl swrh āliikhlāaṣi * "mn qra ql hw āllh aḥd fy mrḍh āldhy ymwt fyh lm yftn fy qbrh wamn mn ḍghṭh ālqbr w ḥmlth ālmlāikh ywm ālqyāmh bakfhā ḥtā tjyzh mn ālṣrāṭ ilā āljnh". (mwḍwʿ) [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/301]. * "mn qra (ql hw āllh aḥd) ʿshryn mrh bnā āllh lh qṣrā fy āljnh". [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/1351]. * "mn qra {ql hw āllh aḥd} māity mrh ghfrt lh dhnwb māity snh". [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/295]. * "mn qra ql hw āllh aḥd māity mrh ktb āllh lh alfā w khmsmāih ḥsnh ilā an ykwn ʿlyh dyn". [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/300]. * "mn mr bālmqābr fqra (ql hw āllh aḥd) iḥdā ʿshrh mrh thm whb ajrh llamwāt aʿṭy mn ālajr bʿdd ālamwāt". [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/1290]. * "mn ṣlā ālṣbḥ thm qra (ql hw āllh aḥd) māih mrh qbl an ytklm fklmā qra (ql hw āllh aḥd) ghfr lh dhnb snh". [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh/405]. * "mn mr ʿlā ālmqābr fqra fyhā iḥdā ʿshrh mrh: {ql hw āllh aḥd} thm whb ajrh llamwāt aʿṭy mn ālajr bʿdd ālamwāt". [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh /3277]. * "mn qra bʿd ṣlāh āljmʿh {ql hw āllh aḥd} w{ql aʿwdh brb ālflq} w{ql aʿwdh brb ālnās} sbʿ mrāt ajārh āllh bhā mn ālswʾ ilā āljmʿh ālakhrā". [slslh ālaḥādyth ālḍʿyfh /4129].
text.fr:
**Mérite de la sourate Al-Ikhlas (Le Monothéisme Pur)** **Hadiths authentiques rapportés sur le mérite de la sourate Al-Ikhlas :** - D'après Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (qu'Allah l'agrée), le Messager d'Allah (paix et salut sur lui) a dit : "Est-ce que l'un de vous est incapable de lire un tiers du Coran chaque nuit ?"
